textByHashes.b2feccd2aca326cd98005f997aa68ba3aae963ff: Well of course I'm alive, silly. I'm Mia Persten! It takes more than the Abyss to kill me.
textByHashes.b34956a94a887d64d38501eb967047d8fe1531ce: Speaking of which, it's a good job I found you. If you'd have got on that boat, you'd have become fish food!
textByHashes.87b2b6462dd5b1c3361641e5dafb9f8e6e032859: Absolutely! Come, I'll explain back at my camp.
textByHashes.d78c53dc7e00ec9b6be1e28041417defdb2bdba6: Welcome to my little home away from home. It's not much, but you take what you can get in a place like this.
textByHashes.e850349c8f1b4c58f4831b2326698fb15c496867: Of course! You won't find anywhere safer in all the Abyss.
textByHashes.e03510f54c3fc5176b90e826917c4456f758856a: Well... that's probably not true, but let's pretend otherwise.
textByHashes.b1cc15169ec187be2f76d9f603a4fd9e7e8ba576: Still, the point is, the demons don't come down here thanks to our fishy friend.
textByHashes.fa1ab1fa7059bc8649412fc575b8cda255236d52: One of the many mysteries of this place. It's some sort of sea creature by the looks of it, but it's nothing like anything I've seen before.
textByHashes.55b47cbfed258283277c558174f4bc3d4e9998d0: It's very dangerous, and it's rather annoyingly preventing any navigation of that lovely lake of goo. Quite unfortunate as there's something out there that I really need to get to.
textByHashes.1456dfc2e5beb147f577567d9214eb133190d567: Leave? But I don't want to leave? I'm a leading expert on teleportation. I'd have had no problem getting out of here if I'd wanted to.
textByHashes.8c29c7812e097ad47f07190bc468a8bac49e424e: Because I need to get past the big fishy, but I can't do it alone. I was hoping someone capable would follow the signal and join me. Looks like it worked perfectly!
textByHashes.149df77e966c2c911f568d1cd4500bac90f2ec4d: There's a big old ship out there that I believe contains something I need.
textByHashes.9d7538597924d2dbb8dc13a80940aa9c9d502c00: Yes! It's her ship I'm trying to reach!
textByHashes.a8f04fe3a55773e2fe3a92beb661e480cd81ff9a: And there I was thinking you'd come here just for me. Consider my feelings hurt. Still, seeing as we both want the same thing, how about we work together?
textByHashes.57a55bbef04079b036bd0b60d2db66825c500bf0: That fish creature needs dealing with. I've already tried myself, but its scales are stronger than anything I've ever encountered before.
textByHashes.601f0364626a0c13faa4d64e699e16ad4768232e: Indeed it does. There's a spot on its back where there's a gap in the scales.
textByHashes.062acc050fe588ed574d4638243f736db78dbef9: The weak spot isn't easy to reach, especially with how quickly the creature can move around the lake. If we want to get close, we'll need to trap it. There's an island next to Perseriya's ship that should work.
textByHashes.3c8251965939cfd4ece7fb7ef742ea553a96b803: I hope you like your fish smoked, because we're going to smoke this one out!
textByHashes.bdd7e767c68245726970efc53722e4fe29b79843: It seems Perseriya was in charge of quite the fleet. Her ship isn't the only one in here. There's plenty of others about, still filled to the brim with all sorts of explosive materials.
textByHashes.e014b2d28246bc5e43ca299c07e83a9e99103663: There's three that I know of, which should be enough for what we need. See those tunnels over there? There's one down each of them.
textByHashes.de1d3d4fab03fa33cc600040246eac067a408973: Two are blocked right now, but I'll get that sorted while you work on the first one. Once you set the ship alight, I'll make sure the smoke is directed right towards our fishy friend.
textByHashes.2bc0da1fb07045afa74b12374f1524eed3e803d5: It wasn't really that hard. Did you not think I would?
textByHashes.e9950f4ac82cb02d01ae1895fbddc14cf9339cbe: Guardians of the Rift? You mentioned them earlier. Who are they?
textByHashes.4b32394f54acedccba4f2ea4100a16e7700b3ef3: Ah, I'm guessing I won't be their favourite person then.
textByHashes.88cca99bba0badffa18347981e5f2868f7ba5cfe: It's not like it was intentional. Besides, we should look on the positive side. I've found all sorts of things in here to help with my research.
textByHashes.7e5b2bbf9077d1d3a1cb8c3ec980564caa33f8b0: Well even if we wanted to, we can't turn back time. We may as well just keep looking forward.
textByHashes.fd20a6f7676da3c7c192be8901b7e9ed048f1800: I reckon I'll find some information there to help with my research.
textByHashes.64402158333d87db155dcc61367810029c7f8e73: If I knew that, I wouldn't need to go there, would I?
textByHashes.7d6e8bda5a2b94552438d4249624d0238129eeba: I found a tablet on another shipwreck down here. It suggested she'd been experimenting with teleportation magic. If that's true, her ship seems the best place to look.
textByHashes.5666bae8c7e16b50d6b6c2bba988ddc77cbac96a: To be honest, I'm not totally sure. It's clear the Abyss has demonic connections, hence, you know, Abyssal Demons. But Black Demons? Greater Demons?
textByHashes.e9c843c22a191881106652f93b5d4b097a62b601: It's obvious they don't belong here. The delightful denizens of the Abyss haven't taken kindly to their presence.
textByHashes.75f95181cbf8156605bda308c2d3a093bf03cfcf: From the rituals they keep performing, my guess is they're looking to harness the magic of the Abyss for themselves.
textByHashes.ed0cdb0c31683ce334225e1502a680738a492137: Any luck with those ships yet?
textByHashes.6268eca17a2fe562ddee4c6918548f842a71c3fa: There should be one down each of those tunnels over there. Two are blocked right now, but I'll get that sorted while you work on the first one.
textByHashes.141434431581feea6479274ecb1f6f8ae8ad38ba: You're back! I'm guessing from all the smoke that came this way that all went as planned?
textByHashes.57d91115448e663510517b67e3ed6ab45dbea71c: Great! I've opened up the second tunnel for you. It's ready when you are.
textByHashes.30f198543234974647046a15b10e6230357d91dd: Really? They must be code names. I wonder what they refer to...
textByHashes.2372d37abbc1e422b26af1d64df2ed674b1972f4: You're starting to sound a bit accusational.
textByHashes.5212ef2b7dfd61b7ed418113bd099ea742b8193b: Do you remember me telling you that my travels had taken me to other planes? One of them was a total wasteland. Ruins of a great civilisation as far as you could see.
textByHashes.c607ca677ff081bfa031ab8d7b7d39236640cf7c: Naturally, me being me, I was curious. I delved deep into the ruins to try and work out what happened.
textByHashes.54d9daec9e2afd9dd7e00069f4ff959f8f4914cd: Turns out, they were in possession of an artefact of immense power. It was capable of limitless creation, but also unimaginable destruction. It delivered them great prosperity, but also led to their ruin.
textByHashes.34b0b6bf3d468ce08a33c6f2af214f42612ededf: I think so. It's been known by many names, and most of them have been long forgotten. The Eye of Saradomin was one. The Fist of Guthix was another. But its true name is...
textByHashes.a3b76d7fdd61bd9f403e49e0a33afda1c5b98871: That's right... How did you know that?
textByHashes.ffbb354da9a72aba7bf8f84608be565186e5afa0: How fascinating... Makes sense though. After all, powerful magic leaves powerful traces.
textByHashes.116538421b017b342b0916b74e8b4898b247a6fe: Yeah... sorry about that. I had a feeling that if I suddenly popped up claiming to have information on a powerful artefact beyond anything ever encountered before, people might not believe me.
textByHashes.c8e5c0234f98aba44a69102100535d0cd46f7600: Well I definitely wouldn't complain, but it's actually this other artefact I'm more interested in. The one referred to as Imperium.
textByHashes.9a3e4035c685445387ad5dd60e14acdc2a620cbe: I always assumed the Stone to be unique, but what if it's not? Could there be other artefacts of a similar power out there? I have to find out!
textByHashes.31b3be708a606cf432f5bc2fc32fad36577c9f4c: And on that note, we should really get back to it. Those ships won't burn themselves!
textByHashes.c24af95157572fb303ac4093ee68fe98fc0c9485: Before you came along, I'd already found quite a few mentions of it hidden in various shipwrecks. It seems that Perseriya possessed it for a time, but it was eventually transported somewhere to the east.
textByHashes.03e2198793603e61c7fb7895624bd8d1e5f57a9d: I don't have anything solid, but multiple times it was compared to the Stone. Even if not directly connected, it seems the two were of a similar type of power.
textByHashes.860674173873ebdb33de2b74df4faf2e4b5dd3e5: Beyond that, I don't really have much. If we want to learn more, we need to reach Perseriya's ship.
textByHashes.7f9dc446861c5b2b75d02d4ff3ba385a52eea0fe: Welcome back. How did things go?
textByHashes.512b2eabccc92653fe21bd608add9d19d448f75d: How exciting! Well don't leave me hanging in anticipation. What did you find?
textByHashes.9cfcf23817ba61715a9e6e340c29eb6ba1861d4d: Ah, that must have been a bit of a bother for them.
textByHashes.5965fa618b459b59cc4f9a14e42b1056082abf74: Oh dear... Still, this is highly useful information. We now know the artefact has the ability to control another, which is equal parts exciting and terrifying!
textByHashes.98211675360be51bddbdd656a38a32e1e0a8a0ba: The question now is, did the artefact do it on its own, or was it enacting the will of the soldier that touched it?
textByHashes.4017ce67a08bc43ee664920f02ee56ecadeaecc3: Indeed! Just one ship to go. I've managed to open up the final tunnel for you.
textByHashes.67b248d50b9763b8ee57366bc85a8b0fcda09437: It's a curious one. We now know Imperium has the power to control another, but that doesn't really tell us much about the overall power of the artefact.
textByHashes.c28c419139b834b7f543b9daa54954cd4754c903: Mind control is a dangerous form of magic. The Order of Wizards forbid it for obvious reasons, but with enough skill, it is possible to learn.
textByHashes.5ecefc57905a58c8f45fd4f778ed05177227c5ca: Mind controlling magic has a large number of drawbacks. All but the most powerful mages can only use it on one person at a time, and the spell is easily broken.
textByHashes.ab05b865bddf016e72e74b9a221ee5b94add49d3: But what if Imperium can overcome these limits? What if it can control hundreds, or even thousands, with no way to break that control!
textByHashes.94b96768a9f8351d00579e979a581c1c4db704dd: First the Stone, and now this 'Imperium'. But that begs the question... how many more? So silly of me! I spent so much time focusing on the Stone, I never even considered the possibility...
textByHashes.6d4d10ac0eb89301ededcf9aedf0f9bb91722f76: Oh, you're back! Sorry, I was in my own little world there, chittering away. Anyway, looks like our plan worked. I was able to use all that smoke to force our big fish towards that island!
textByHashes.d8a1ccd434ee3e2896000ac48bbf52a7f8013c04: That's right! Now don't worry, I have a very in-depth plan. Basically, one of us needs to be ready to direct the smoke in case it tries to escape while the other goes and gives it a good old poke!
textByHashes.fff899cbce2bc9130c8dc4064090373e5ba7b6de: Do you have the magical abilities to maintain a huge whirlwind of smoke that's strong enough to prevent a large aquatic lifeform from passing through?
textByHashes.1f7b83d5e47753f89aecacdc67aa797ceb637d1c: Then yes, you'll be doing the poking!
textByHashes.ef43f9c023637513c02fe94804c85d6343fed136: Absolutely! Oh, don't forget there's a weak spot on its back that you'll want to try and target. I can't see it letting you get too close, so I reckon you'll want to try and stun it first.
textByHashes.b55e31550bf0c5c17ae54943a8a0801b638b1d6f: From my experiences in this place, some sort of shadowy spell should do the trick! I'm sure a capable individual like you has access to something suitable.
textByHashes.c3d5fab85c7ee93900041635422e3b170d0d6c2a: Because I'm Mia Persten, silly! You don't get as far as me without knowing a thing or two about forbidden magic. Now, just hop on the boat and row out to the island when you're ready. Oh, and try not to die!
textByHashes.dbc53c3f5c9458f6c4cdba082ec818c276105c71: That big fish won't go sorting itself out. Just hop on the boat and row out to the island when you're ready.
textByHashes.265756fcec394127a2f4b043e8da3cc9c044f1fa: Alright, let's see what we've got...
textByHashes.3923ba3c4beec4d091ce4738d27aa72e2257b549: It can't be possible... can it?
textByHashes.d7b40515d83038ac322f4a9106f16429505f5210: It's written by one of the crew on Perseriya's ship. Apparently they snuck into her private cabin...
textByHashes.6644f4dda55f282ce3b976ea188950ad8397e5d3: This was after Imperium had already been delivered to the east!
textByHashes.db92133d95b9819cecd7bde4169f8c26acbc083e: Exactly! That either means she was able to sneak it back onto the ship again, or there was a second artefact!
textByHashes.325ff73d542a0c905853706da46ebd04e640c953: I don't know... I don't think so... But then again, until recently I didn't even know about this artefact at all.
textByHashes.b888d9812d7aa1079352c28b19ae23d152468c9b: I'm not too sure. I need to gather all my research and see what I can come up with. I guess that means that I've outlived my little home here.
textByHashes.8c75bc12c1ea86b9e6d1f473300b04168cf85b99: Well... maybe not just yet. I'll have to face the music there at some point, but probably best I wait until after I've got my head around all this Imperium business.
textByHashes.ceb7f9b7ae584808df666cc2424051540b2ae918: If you don't mind, I'd appreciate it if you'd not mention what we found here to Sedridor and the others until I'm ready.
textByHashes.74e0e4e1e50a646274618c4973d270e9b84711de: Oh don't worry, I will. Now, I'd best be getting off. I'm sure I'll see you again before long. I hope you find what you're looking for here.
textByHashes.ad3d33a283fb2aab60abf3865e174a8fa0dcb894: Don't you remember how we survived the horrors of the Scar?
textByHashes.c27d772dcd70f5391400b0eae4c5c2e4fa88b814: What? You haven't heard of me? The great Mia Persten of the Order of Wizards? The leading researcher in teleportation magic? The creator of the Home Teleport spell?
textByHashes.2fd9fe082a582119ee5456e84b153e1506129c4d: No offence taken! My research tends to take me far from the Wizards' Tower. I've been all across Gielinor and even to some other planes of existence!
textByHashes.051f94ccf541969e60ed558431b2997fd32bc187: Should I not be in Al Kharid?
textByHashes.ac3cc2bf9a109793a8629b087158038f9eba90d6: Well, maybe you should start expecting the unexpected! That way you can spend less time worrying about why I'm here, and more time asking life's real questions.
textByHashes.1aabe23358abe0090627b1ddb146453c1c9b0d44: Why shouldn't a wizard be in Al Kharid?
textByHashes.aaeae417c50b1a299cfc0154af24d3888490dfef: Well for a start, I'm not just any wizard. The name's Mia Persten. I'm the leading researcher in teleportation magic for the Order of Wizards.
textByHashes.39b1d763c3358516f8b4830970dad1ea4d7838ed: Now as for your question, maybe you should start expecting the unexpected! That way you can spend less time worrying about why I'm here, and more time asking life's real questions.
textByHashes.d7d285206f1adcaa59ef2abd8bdfa875b00ff1e4: Well, that's the thing. It's different for everyone! Why do trees grow from stumps substantially faster than from seeds? Where do meteorites come from? Why do we have to hold our arms out to teleport successfully?
textByHashes.2f47e4e47ac3a3d88fd40fce80c1f0a12080c7ca: No, silly! I came here to buy something.
textByHashes.d15f7db925520cc0905090f55d091c7bc8e63e4d: That's right! Something! Tell me, what do you know of runecrafting?
textByHashes.69cbc8b04a2bc29b410e575bfd0ab7846a9874fa: A shame. Unfortunately, this something requires some understanding in that area.
textByHashes.b5fd08931ade9bda837e510efa0f25e432d0a749: That's right! Something! Tell me, what do you know of the Abyss?
textByHashes.947a96ed6505dee4f340309e3772c6f83e33d6c8: A shame. Unfortunately, this something requires somewhat of an abyssal understanding.
textByHashes.6e373e7c2a051287f0b20dbbb915f803b3119801: Really? It's quite unusual for an adventurer like yourself to have knowledge in that area.
textByHashes.fe59e56d78d930de9b307672ba66188c5dc89dae: Zamorakian wizards? You do realise that the Z.M.I. are enemies to the Order of Wizards, right?
textByHashes.80e0fb044d41f110a76f13262678316ff4615f36: Oh, don't worry about it. The Order of Wizards may be a Saradominist group, but I've never personally had much time for gods. I prefer knowledge to religion.
textByHashes.e02fdecde6a5d724a14d2a8f8ed76a51d28b4544: Still, it's maybe best you don't tell anyone else. I can't imagine my fellow wizards will be happy to know that you gave away details on their secret mine.
textByHashes.188211fc98b7fa217622480340bb475c2dde0852: Enough about them though. You've just shared quite the revelation with me! Is it really true that the Z.M.I. have found a way to access the Abyss?
textByHashes.a4f01341e4768936517a778f971908aa757747cc: Well in that case, I think you might be the perfect person to aid me in a little task.
textByHashes.50205a4c2b6a212cf20caea3512f2f0730622086: Oh, don't be silly! This will be fun!
textByHashes.bf6513a7ed4ae9a6be68a4b6836532bb44492640: Well I can't go telling you that until you agree, can I? Wouldn't want you sharing my secrets.
textByHashes.4ab56dc0dbf02615c4274937a982e1f5fbeaf7e3: There'll be quite the reward for you at the end!
textByHashes.fcab465ad6fa597ec43df919da98ab5b189b6249: That's a shame. Well, you just let me know if you reconsider.
textByHashes.8737ac89249c7555ab4e6f3501955e0745de33d2: Hello again! Have you changed your mind about helping me with that little task?
textByHashes.97a522177235a9234fd14763620ef4b0046626ed: You'll help? Great! Well first, a question for you: you say the Z.M.I. have gained access to the Abyss? By any chance, are they using it to gain quick access to the runic altars for runecrafting purposes?
textByHashes.0558c362a479db130b1eb128ad14b99925cbb41b: I knew it! I've been wondering for some time how they've been creating so many runes. I'd never considered that they'd somehow gained access to the Abyss, but I now realise it was the obvious explanation.
textByHashes.2c5793e62155f6bb906add1c65e722cd64e6d8e8: Well, yes, but actually, no. It seems we've jumped ahead a bit. Let's go back to why I'm here.
textByHashes.13448464c5331a162b2ac0787f90af7ddcac9eca: The 'something' I bought! Here, take a look.
textByHashes.cbf63c29630a1059efa36598e425e224a5de494e: A foolish archaeologist by the name of Asgarnia Smith recently found this washed up near the Ruins of Uzer. Despite his apparent archaeological credentials, he dismissed it as junk.
textByHashes.4ea1d4519d5c5c38dc967731a0748508f71fa895: Luckily, I was able to buy it from him before he disposed of it. Can you believe he thought the 100 coins I paid was too much? If only he knew the true value of something like this...
textByHashes.08f7f08de06a46243065ed937067dcbc24d0783e: You see that eye on it? That is the symbol of both the Order of Wizards and our home, the Wizards' Tower. Thing is, that's only the start.
textByHashes.20524ce6bf6de6a39c7f64e58cc545855f7f1735: From what I can tell, this amulet is thousands of years old, but our order was only formed at the dawn of the Fifth Age. That wasn't even two hundred years ago!
textByHashes.fc771cef7aa701f94710e009550a10752e4f1f31: Hang on! I'm not done yet. I also inspected the amulet for magical energies and found something fascinating! I recognised two distinct magical signatures.
textByHashes.07dff6b02d4d1b2d6192c883ce9d335d08ec5f63: The first is the same magic found in rune essence. That's already pretty exciting, but it's the second that will blow your mind!
textByHashes.1f1ca04e9d3793cb2204ca659c035bef2758abea: I'm getting to that! My, you do like interrupting, don't you? Don't worry, I understand. I get quite excited about these things as well!
textByHashes.7bc98e497bce5e44e6f6b746f250f372d22ba9a6: Anyway, here's the big reveal for you: the second signature was energy from the Abyss!
textByHashes.ce3a3d7da3eb2e7628f45b18f40deb7523bf42fe: Now you see why this is so exciting!
textByHashes.5bcf70ca1ec8be97d844a193f9dae756ef6dbaac: I need to find out where this amulet came from. It should be possible to trace it using the abyssal energy it still contains.
textByHashes.f716dab5b0206c08af833ba4293326a03d7a88a4: This is where you ask 'What's the catch?'
textByHashes.1ca79225640b0b0c670d242705bc24d6ac47d491: I'm glad you asked, ! Unfortunately, the spell needed to trace the amulet will only work properly from inside the Abyss.
textByHashes.8cd9d06a11abced993ed512ff2d764df5a450773: Precisely! While I don't care about the religious squabbling between the Z.M.I. and my order, I still can't see them wanting to work with me any time soon.
textByHashes.f1881bdb5c2d9bf9ae842da4aad14c610064660c: Correct! I imagine you'll need the help of your Z.M.I. friends.
textByHashes.d1bafddd28c12c144b7a7f550b01dd622e36e151: Just maybe don't tell them who gave it to you.
textByHashes.fbe0c348c1f01c57a39c0d79b045be3b6b2ac3c4: Return to me once you are done. Oh, and don't forget the amulet!
textByHashes.636df0d6909a35712955f7309dff7d204a2acd7a: Hello again! Here, take this amulet to your Z.M.I. friend in Varrock. Hopefully he can tell us where it came from.
textByHashes.22e056bcd5c457dad910b95ebefaa0349713b89e: Hello again! Any luck with that amulet?
textByHashes.4b32566846176d2011edd9ad7ac48544802e075b: You lost a one of a kind ancient artefact?
textByHashes.191060c49c1e2bc82344ee66a208f83857e556ee: Well, good job I found it again. Here you go. Take it to your Z.M.I. friend in Varrock. Hopefully he can tell us where it came from.
textByHashes.1b99d66b9a57a7c09efb9cee23d23bfea4237703: You should take it to your Z.M.I. friend in Varrock. Hopefully he can tell us where it came from.
textByHashes.a23bf4af253726f207a01e0f20461ebff7a37ae4: Oh, fascinating! Hmm... But that would mean...
textByHashes.67c450e1fef3200d62e71a59ea239532f4de5a84: Yes... Two... no three more! Silly me! Counted myself didn't I.
textByHashes.28914d4798670ddfe6a5f9bc399720efee6218d2: Well it should be fine. I can't see them saying no. After all, it's their symbol as well...
textByHashes.dc26e76acf2a7808b533ed00e5e35f1b4c691ec7: Oh yes, you're here! Brilliant! You'll be pleased to know that I've worked out what we must do next.
textByHashes.6e42fa84c33d714eda3aee32ed5d6fc1abbe8197: Patience, young adventurer. Before we get to that, let me share with you my theory on where this amulet came from.
textByHashes.6a92dc65869ed57e4a74480113b4609683b90c18: While you were gone, I was thinking about why the amulet would have magical energy from both rune essence and the Abyss. All of a sudden, it hit me!
textByHashes.3108a6dbb86eb1a3390bcbab2664f7c65b80cc66: Quite the opposite! The source of this amulet must be an ancient teleportation matrix to the runic altars, just like the one the Z.M.I. have in the Abyss!
textByHashes.14c3072bedfe79418c62d4e0590a055fa379768b: This is good news, in case you were wondering! Access to a teleportation matrix like that will be life changing when it comes to my work!
textByHashes.02564b3bff31790f1b90f17bbd3c76d1639cdd56: Of course, the rest of my order will be very happy as well. It'll completely remove the advantage the Z.M.I. currently have. Now there's a balance that Guthix himself would love!
textByHashes.28e9330a19ed055476a80b3a9cf8f5d6cadefd41: Ah, but this talk of the Z.M.I. has reminded me of something. Could I have that amulet back?
textByHashes.77f6d9438ed5e723c16f38acca4f380e975ff54e: Ah, but this talk of the Z.M.I. has reminded me of something. You lost that amulet didn't you?
textByHashes.142e2a4cd3508fc32daa73e877a9a7aa18e3ffd7: Don't worry, I found it for you. I'll keep hold of it for now.
textByHashes.4c111c09579e50c835f35501c8745dec0b7e1733: Now, just give me a moment...
textByHashes.d1755caf874288321b7471aaa414b3508282f34a: Ah, yes. Well, that's not surprising.
textByHashes.132d2104d51857313ed3f6d77544c74e0921c659: Your Z.M.I. friends cast a transmission spell on the amulet. Had we taken it with us, it would have transmitted all sorts of information back to them about the origin.
textByHashes.545251eb01821f0a005544df130fa893b105379b: It's not a problem. I suspected they might do something like this. The good news is that this means they have no intention of using that incantation themselves.
textByHashes.ce4da04eb8c8b87208f2b742a0f38aea418c3104: Again, as expected. They prefer to lurk in the shadows. To be honest, I totally understand. I've been known to do the same from time to time.
textByHashes.48e21c12d262c6a2a5d618cf35a6f687ed6a1a3d: Anyway, I've modified their spell to send false information to them. They'll be none the wiser.
textByHashes.93abb2420e96db3951c4ffde36f24d1045069b85: Oh, my friend, you misunderstand. I have no issues with the Z.M.I. having the information, but I don't want them getting it before I've even had a chance to determine what it is!
textByHashes.0d04444e0fba2e71b65aa4e3c5ea6016cde8d1a1: Yes, back to business! The incantation is quite complex, so we will need the help of three other wizards to perform it. I'm afraid I'm not including you in that, although you are very talented!
textByHashes.05f2fb005a41986fdaba8db08436c64bcd7d46af: From the Wizards' Tower of course! My fellow wizards won't be able to resist getting their hands on a teleportation matrix, especially one that may be connected to the history of our order!
textByHashes.58b35584e769ab81822a4d8f5ad215f1ded019dc: Now, you head to the Wizards' Tower and show that incantation to Sedridor. I have a couple of things to prepare first, but I will join you there soon.
textByHashes.687eba2d468b0a3c8d1874fd52cdd70d985089db: Oh, and whatever you do, don't mention the Z.M.I. while you're there.
textByHashes.c406af889a0763700c0a396732af89c53f6d596f: Hey, if you want, I can teleport you to the Wizards' Tower. Consider it a one-time gift from me.
textByHashes.76cb7b3ee87b1d0a0d253cc6e2a1b6fe12949466: Well let me know if you change your mind.
textByHashes.15dda3bcd0dee012bd5105aee039733437402c31: Hello again! Did you take that incantation to Sedridor?
textByHashes.16c669f6df26505dd57e905fdb5c5955307dc029: No matter, I can give you a copy of mine. Try not to lose it this time!
textByHashes.e1371e36c5e6227a046948fa42cee00e758670e6: Now quickly! Time is of the essence! If you want, I can teleport you to the Wizards' Tower. Consider it a one-time gift from me.
textByHashes.5001eca764fda86ca1464534a85fd7ff8e7743ba: Well hurry up! If you want, I can teleport you to the Wizards' Tower. Consider it a one-time gift from me.
textByHashes.e2f5d7d1f75ea3069f361da49cd0a8482b8be0c6: Now quickly! Time is of the essence!
textByHashes.1be7a0e7468293bc236e8a2fa7fbb8372341c347: Well hurry up! This could be the biggest discovery of our lifetimes!
textByHashes.6aeca7aebc9e382a20238cc36836b3b78e52136a: Hello again! Ready to uncover a magical secret that could change the world forever?
textByHashes.2aab6c5c0573d5da4005cbd1e6af6c994a43d11a: That's right! The amulet is as well, of course, but Sedridor here likes to be careful.
textByHashes.45796d9a39092efca1bea56d1b4c98c4d0e295a6: The unfortunate price of progress, Sedridor.
textByHashes.358fabb9f7069c6c7db31bb0d3a6b3b739d98798: Well then what are we waiting for? Let's do this!
textByHashes.55cb05e95bb8d3637c58f2ea28f40560dd175f8f: It's going to be quite something! You really should let them help!
textByHashes.fb9b6b53d068ff93d79cc547171323dbbd96f904: This place is brilliant! The apprentices are looking around to see what they can discover. You should join them!
textByHashes.d786d03c150f7d00a51e7c662ff99ae1a02b0e9f: Oh, sorry, I didn't see you there. I was lost in thought!
textByHashes.7a2a8e35f773b37e1624c0b1d0fdeafbab54bbc4: Hmm? Oh, I was looking at these markings here. They're very interesting! They seem to be radiating a magical energy I've never encountered before!
textByHashes.720177e40006886bcd9c1a119481e3b9d6606365: Take a closer look! Maybe you'll feel the energy as well!
textByHashes.a1209ca1b5c6382c77c674fcb1e3f91be068f149: Well, time to see what the others have found!
textByHashes.b96d295f598b692bf8a0dd800b00aa1af49bcb1e: Hey! Let's see what the others have found!
textByHashes.aaa6edbb0778e49e128de15c5b6b0444dad087de: You sure can! What else do we have?
textByHashes.8334ce0d7b7d4e57d1d2b58add17943f7e98f769: So this place was definitely used by wizards at one point. Amazing!
textByHashes.00edb380c7a160f5a234670c4741778ed38253ea: Remarkable! The justiciars haven't been around for thousands of years. That confirms my suspicions about the age of this place.
textByHashes.56ad8f48bc7d1d305786d9f29c5b64c7313f2baf: Sorry... made by Saradomin, you say?
textByHashes.3b1b0889267fddd052cd3597a67dc9c33db70cc7: Oh, just checking! I get all caught up in the moment and mishear things sometimes.
textByHashes.49f107609bd8d22ec6abd62c5f5e58abe4bcdf1f: Ah, sorry! Lost in thought again! It was for a good reason though. I think I've just about worked it all out!
textByHashes.e65780718d19c66aad7b7ba3e899d58a78863fee: Thank you! So, I reckon my theory that this is an old teleportation matrix was spot on! The wizards here must have used power from the Abyss to create portals to the runic altars.
textByHashes.5504babbbc1ffe56e08e6555fe432a15fc4f2a22: Then, with the power from this 'Eye' and support from their rune guardians, they'd have been able to create runes at lightning speed!
textByHashes.98333f5f8d93c07b9f28dc952e646dac3ca05d9c: The Eye must have had a huge impact on their work. They named the temple after it and fashioned a symbol based on it.
textByHashes.345d40b20fc13f94a2d9b0e00051a025fc8c7c5a: In a moment. I'm just going to cast a simple revealing spell first, just in case there's anything else hidden here.
textByHashes.24bf9bcd02e1985c4f0a96398ef0c274feee6c13: Don't worry! I'm sure everything is fine. Let me handle this.
textByHashes.909c2a9ac51a0c4a1987a72b2377e3f1fe97b1e1: Huh... now that is a problem.
